threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say ‘thank you’ to the bus driver. True locals just wave and keep moving on.
Last night on the 59, a guy serenaded the whole bus with his guitar. Must've been 3 a.m. and somehow it felt like a private gig.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I caught the scent of curry and grilled sausage mingling in the air at the Turkish market on Hermannstraße. That’s when it hit me.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find abandoned subway stations turned into art spaces. The weird murals down at ‘Pankow’ are a must-see for anyone curious.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know to avoid the 16th Street BART station on Fridays after 5—it's a complete madhouse with overheated train brakes and gridlock.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy juggling on the bus while the driver was stuck in traffic. Everyone was more amused than annoyed. Just another day in the city!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the parks, but it's the back streets buzzing with late-night cervezas that show the real spirit.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they look confusedly at the bus timetable—like it’s a puzzle instead of a schedule. No one takes the time to understand it, right?

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You call it a 'shop' and not a 'corner shop', and your face lights up at the sight of 'local' artisan bread. They'll know you’re fresh off the bus.
Last night on the 35, a bloke serenaded us all with a ukulele. Smelled like takeaway and freedom. Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a street artist painting a mural while a kebab shop’s smoke mingled with the scent of fresh bagels from the bakery next door.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a club buried inside an old sausage factory, complete with neon lights and a dance floor that used to be the meat locker.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here if they still know which taco truck’s salsa packs the best heat, even after a BART derailment.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the 38 bus line with an opera aria. All the riders paused, phones out, and for a moment, the chaos faded. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all bars close by 2 am, but many hidden locals stay open well past dawn, alive with energy and laughter.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their reluctance to step off the sidewalk when a pedestrian light blinks yellow—locals are always ready to dodge traffic.
